title: Disorders of Consciousness Assessment: Joint Roles of Ignition and Interoception
description: This clinical biomarker study examines whether interoceptive precision (indexed by HEP amplitude) and ignition capacity (indexed by the Perturbational Complexity Index, PCI) jointly predict clinical recovery outcomes across disorders of consciousness (DoC) better than either biomarker alone. Four sub-predictions are tested: (Pred 4.A) HEP + PCI joint models outperform univariate predictors of DoC recovery, with ΔR² ≥ 0.05 as the primary confirmatory threshold and AUC > 0.80 as an aspirational secondary metric only; (Pred 4.B) HEP amplitude confirms a four-group ordinal gradient (VS/UWS < MCS < EMCS < controls) with DMN connectivity criteria; (Pred 4.C) interoceptive perturbation increases PCI ≥ 10% in MCS patients but not VS/UWS, with perturbation-evoked ΔPCI exceeding arousal-matched control by ≥ 5%, and PCI change correlated with HEP change; (Pred 4.D) HEP amplitude correlates with GCS-S score at 3-month follow-up (confirmatory; 6-month is exploratory pending OSF amendment) with PPV ≥ 70% and NPV ≥ 70% for 6-month recovery status. Directly supported by Paper 3 Appendix DoC Table.
prediction_cluster_label: Joint Roles of Ignition and Interoception in Disorders of Consciousness

sub_predictions.claim: Joint HEP + PCI model explains significantly more variance in 3-month GCS-S outcome than either biomarker alone.
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: ΔR² ≥ 0.05 over best univariate model (primary confirmatory threshold); LRT p < 0.05; AUC > 0.80 (aspirational secondary metric only).
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: Joint model R² ≤ max(univariate HEP R², univariate PCI R²); or ΔR² < 0.05 in primary analysis.

confirming_thresholds.secondary_metric_note: AUC > 0.80 is aspirational secondary metric ONLY — not required for confirmation

falsifying_thresholds.condition_1: Joint model R² ≤ max(univariate HEP R², univariate PCI R²)

sub_predictions.claim: HEP amplitude discriminates MCS from VS/UWS at group level; four-group gradient (VS/UWS < MCS < EMCS < controls) confirmed for both HEP and PCI.
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: HEP amplitude MCS > VS/UWS (Mann–Whitney p < 0.05, d > 0.5); four-group ordinal gradient confirmed for both HEP and PCI; DMN–PCI r > 0.5; DMN–HEP partial correlation below DMN–PCI.
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: No significant HEP difference between MCS and VS/UWS; four-group gradient absent for HEP or PCI.

confirming_thresholds.dmn_hep_constraint: DMN–HEP partial correlation must be below DMN–PCI r

falsifying_thresholds.condition_1: No significant HEP difference between MCS and VS/UWS
falsifying_thresholds.condition_2: Four-group gradient absent for HEP or PCI

sub_predictions.claim: Interoceptive perturbation increases PCI in MCS patients but not VS/UWS; perturbation-evoked ΔPCI exceeds arousal-matched control.
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: PCI increase ≥ 10% in MCS post-perturbation (paired t p < 0.05); VS/UWS group shows no significant change; ΔPCI_perturbation > ΔPCI_control + 5% (paired t p < 0.05); PCI change correlates with HEP change (r > 0.4).
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: No significant PCI change post-perturbation in MCS (ΔPCI < 5%, p > 0.10); or equivalent PCI change in VS/UWS and MCS; or perturbation-evoked PCI change statistically indistinguishable from arousal-matched control (p > 0.10).

confirming_thresholds.vsuws_no_change: VS/UWS shows no significant PCI change

falsifying_thresholds.condition_2: Equivalent PCI change in VS/UWS and MCS
falsifying_thresholds.condition_3: ΔPCI_perturbation statistically indistinguishable from arousal-matched control (p > 0.10)

sub_predictions.claim: HEP amplitude correlates with GCS-S score at 3-month follow-up (primary confirmatory); 6-month follow-up is exploratory pending OSF amendment.
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: Within-participant Spearman r(HEP, GCS-S) > 0.4 at 3-month follow-up (confirmatory); 6-month follow-up reported as exploratory pending OSF amendment. Joint baseline model PPV ≥ 70% and NPV ≥ 70% for 6-month recovery status.
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: HEP shows no significant longitudinal correlation with GCS-S (r < 0.2) at 3-month follow-up; or joint model predictive values do not exceed clinical benchmarks.

confirming_thresholds.followup_6mo_status: EXPLORATORY — pending OSF amendment before analysis

validation_notes: Formally specified. Directly supported by Paper 3 Appendix (Disorders of Consciousness Table). EMCS group added to n_per_group, four-group gradient added to Pred 4.B, DMN criteria added, Pred 4.C three-criterion structure added, and Pred 4.D 6-month exploratory flag and PPV/NPV added to match registry EP-4 requirements.

participants.emcs_note: EMCS group required for four-group gradient confirmation (Pred 4.B). If insufficient EMCS patients are recruited (n < 5), Pred 4.B four-group gradient is reported as exploratory with ≥ 3 groups confirmatory.
inclusion_criteria: Clinical DoC diagnosis by certified neurologist using CRS-R and GCS-S
Stable medical condition permitting EEG and TMS assessment
Etiology: traumatic or hypoxic-ischemic brain injury
exclusion_criteria: Active seizure disorder or anti-epileptic drug modification within 2 weeks
Significant cardiac arrhythmia precluding reliable ECG R-peak detection
Metallic implants precluding TMS (for PCI sub-study)
healthy_controls_criteria: No history of neurological or psychiatric disorder
Age- and sex-matched to patient groups

apparatus.EEG: 64-channel BrainAmp DC (Brain Products GmbH) at 1000 Hz, bedside-compatible cap
apparatus.ECG: BrainAmp ExG bipolar lead-II synchronised to EEG
apparatus.TMS_for_PCI: Magstim Rapid² with 70 mm figure-of-eight coil; vertex stimulation protocol for PCI
apparatus.interoceptive_stimulation: Heartbeat attention task: auditory feedback of participant's own heartbeat via stethoscope and speaker, 5-minute block
apparatus.arousal_matched_control: Auditory noise stimulation (matched duration and intensity to interoceptive condition, non-heartbeat-synchronised) for Pred 4.C ΔPCI_control
apparatus.dmn_connectivity: Resting-state EEG source analysis (sLORETA or beamforming) for DMN proxy; or resting fMRI if clinically feasible (Pred 4.B DMN criteria)

target.type: TMS pulse for PCI; auditory heartbeat for interoceptive stimulation condition; auditory noise for arousal-matched control condition

trial_structure.response: Passive EEG recording; no behavioural response required from patients. Controls perform auditory detection task.

sessions.baseline: Enrolment (week 0): EEG resting state + PCI + CRS-R + GCS-S + DMN proxy
sessions.stimulation: Week 1: Interoceptive stimulation block + post-stimulation PCI + arousal-matched control block + post-control PCI
sessions.followup_3mo: Month 3: EEG + PCI + CRS-R + GCS-S (PRIMARY CONFIRMATORY for Pred 4.D)
sessions.followup_6mo: Month 6: EEG + PCI + CRS-R + GCS-S (EXPLORATORY — requires OSF amendment before analysis)

eeg_analysis_pipeline.ica_cardiac_artifact: Automated ICA cardiac component rejection (EEGLAB ADJUST)
eeg_analysis_pipeline.pci_computation_software: Comolatti et al. (2019) open-source toolbox; PCI threshold for consciousness = 0.31
eeg_analysis_pipeline.dmn_analysis: Resting-state connectivity: ICA-derived DMN component or seed-based connectivity (PCC seed); correlation with HEP amplitude and PCI per participant

dependent_variables: HEP amplitude (250–400 ms, Cz) per DoC group and time point — primary (Pred 4.A, 6.B, 6.D)
PCI per DoC group and time point — primary (Pred 4.A, 6.B)
ΔR² (joint HEP + PCI model vs. best univariate model) for 3-month GCS-S — PRIMARY Pred 4.A
AUC (joint model) for binary recovery outcome — aspirational secondary Pred 4.A only
Univariate model R² for HEP alone and PCI alone (comparison benchmarks for Pred 4.A)
Four-group ordinal gradient: HEP and PCI across VS/UWS, MCS, EMCS, controls — Pred 4.B
DMN–HEP partial correlation (r, controlling for PCI) — Pred 4.B
ΔPCI post-interoceptive perturbation: MCS group (% change from baseline) — Pred 4.C
ΔPCI post-arousal-matched control: MCS group (% change) — Pred 4.C comparison
Spearman r(HEP amplitude, GCS-S) at 3-month follow-up within-participant — confirmatory Pred 4.D
PPV and NPV: joint model prediction of 6-month recovery status — exploratory Pred 4.D
Spearman r(HEP amplitude, GCS-S) at 6-month follow-up — exploratory Pred 4.D only
primary_hypothesis: A linear model including both HEP amplitude and PCI at enrolment explains ΔR² ≥ 0.05 more variance in 3-month GCS-S outcome than either biomarker alone. HEP amplitude and PCI show a four-group gradient (VS/UWS < MCS < EMCS < controls), interoceptive perturbation increases PCI ≥ 10% in MCS but not VS/UWS with ΔPCI exceeding arousal-matched control by ≥ 5%, and HEP amplitude correlates with 3-month GCS-S (Spearman r > 0.4).
primary_analysis: Pred 4.A: Multiple linear regression GCS-S_3mo ~ HEP + PCI + age + etiology; LRT vs. univariate models; ΔR² as primary metric. Pred 4.B: Mann–Whitney U (MCS vs. VS/UWS); ordinal trend test across four groups (Jonckheere–Terpstra); DMN–PCI and DMN–HEP correlations. Pred 4.C: paired t-tests for ΔPCI (perturbation vs. baseline; perturbation vs. control); Pearson r for PCI–HEP change. Pred 4.D: Wilcoxon signed-rank for longitudinal HEP–GCS-S correlation; PPV/NPV from binary recovery classification.

power_analysis.based_on: Rosanova et al. (2018) PCI in DoC; Canales-Johnson et al. (2020) HEP group differences

pre_registration.amendment_required_for_6mo: OSF amendment must be submitted and time-stamped before 6-month data are analysed for Pred 4.D exploratory analysis

ethics.committee: Institutional Review Board and Research Ethics Committee (clinical)
ethics.surrogate_consent: Required for all patient participants; surrogate (next-of-kin or legal guardian) provides written informed consent

caveats: PCI requires TMS, which cannot be applied to patients with metallic implants or unstable hemodynamics; PCI subsample may be smaller than full cohort.

HEP reliability in DoC populations is lower than in healthy controls due to cardiac artifact overlap; minimum 20 clean epochs per participant required; participants below this threshold excluded from HEP analyses.
Etiology (traumatic vs. hypoxic-ischemic) is a known moderator of recovery trajectory and is included as covariate in all models.
EMCS group (Pred 4.B four-group gradient) may be undersampled; if n < 5 EMCS, four-group confirmatory test is downgraded to exploratory and three-group (VS/UWS, MCS, controls) is primary.
Pred 4.D 6-month follow-up is EXPLORATORY: analysis must not begin until OSF amendment is submitted and time-stamped. PPV/NPV reported for this window only.
